name: persistent shared memory
description: A memory system maintained across multiple agents that enables knowledge reuse and collaboration in multi-agent systems.

name: asynchronous multi-agent execution
description: A coordination approach where multiple agents operate independently and concurrently rather than synchronously.

name: autonomous agents
description: Agents capable of self-directed exploration, reflection, and decision-making without relying on fixed heuristics or hard-coded rules.

name: knowledge reuse
description: The ability of agents to leverage previously discovered insights and solutions across multiple iterations of exploration.

name: multi-agent collaboration
description: The process by which multiple agents work together to explore and improve solutions through communication and shared learning.

name: open-ended discovery
description: A domain where progress requires sustained search and knowledge accumulation without predetermined endpoints or solutions.

name: LLM-based evolution
description: Using language models to iteratively improve solutions through evolution-inspired search processes.

name: heartbeat-based interventions
description: A control mechanism that periodically checks and adjusts agent behavior at regular intervals.

name: kernel engineering optimization
description: A systems optimization task focused on improving computational kernel performance measured in execution cycles.

name: Dash
description: A self-learning multi-agent data system that handles routine data queries, writes SQL, and improves through captured corrections and learned patterns.

name: AgentOS
description: An agent platform providing interface for chatting with agents, viewing sessions, traces, metrics, and schedules.

name: Ashpreet Bedi
description: Creator and author of Dash, a multi-agent data system for companies.

name: Dual-tier knowledge system
description: A knowledge architecture combining curated metadata, validated queries, and business rules with automatically captured learnings and corrections.

name: Schema isolation strategy
description: A separation strategy that isolates validated query patterns and business logic from raw schema dumps to ensure agent reliability.

name: Self-learning data system
description: A system that learns from corrections, data quirks, and repeated patterns to improve its accuracy and build infrastructure over time.

name: Leader agent
description: An agent that routes requests, synthesizes answers, and triggers infrastructure building when it detects repeated patterns.

name: Analyst agent
description: A read-only agent that searches knowledge, writes SQL, and runs queries against the database.

name: Engineer agent
description: An agent with write access to the dash schema that builds views, summary tables, and computed data infrastructure.

name: PgVector
description: A vector database extension backing Dash's curated knowledge system for semantic search.

name: SQLAlchemy
description: A Python library used in Dash to intercept and validate SQL statements before execution.

name: Knowledge system
description: Dash's repository of table metadata, validated queries, business rules, and learned corrections used to guide agent decision-making.

name: Captured corrections
description: Automatic recording of corrections, data quirks, and fixes discovered during agent execution to improve future queries.

name: Validated queries
description: Pre-validated SQL patterns with correct JOINs, NULL handling, and edge case management stored in the knowledge system.

name: Business rules
description: Domain-specific context about how metrics are calculated and data constraints that distinguish correct answers from plausible-looking wrong ones.

name: Dash
description: A self-learning data agent built with systems engineering principles that grounds answers in 6 layers of context and improves with every query.

name: AgentOS
description: A web UI and platform for deploying and managing AI agents with authentication and integration capabilities.

name: Self-learning loop
description: A systematic process that captures error patterns and learnings to improve agent performance over time without requiring model updates.

name: Context system
description: A multi-layer architecture combining schema annotations, query patterns, learnings, computed fields, and health checks to provide structured context for agents.

name: Knowledge base
description: A data-driven repository of validated queries, business rules, and institutional knowledge that agents reliably query and refine.

name: Learning Machine
description: An automated system that captures and manages error patterns and fixes without requiring manual curation.

name: Agent memory
description: Systematic storage of error patterns, type gotchas, and discovered fixes that agents can reference to avoid repeating mistakes.

name: Dual schema enforcement
description: A structural pattern separating company data (read-only public schema) from agent-managed data (writable dash schema) for safety and clarity.

name: Analyst agent
description: An agent team member that reads public and dash schema data to answer questions using validated queries and reasoning tools.

name: Engineer agent
description: An agent team member that reads public schema and writes to dash schema to build reusable data assets and update knowledge bases.

name: RBAC
description: A security model that controls agent and user access to resources based on assigned roles and permissions.

name: SQLTools
description: A tool interface providing read-only or full database access for agents to query and modify data.

name: ReasoningTools
description: Tools that help agents reason about intent, diagnose errors, and generate grounded outputs.

name: code-review-graph
description: Local knowledge graph for Claude Code that reduces token spend in LLM-driven code review through blast-radius computation via static analysis and Tree-sitter parsing.

name: Tree-sitter
description: Parser generator tool that builds abstract syntax trees from source code with grammar support for 19+ programming languages.

name: MCP
description: Standard protocol for connecting AI assistants to external tools and data sources via structured context exchange.

name: Claude Code
description: Anthropic's integrated AI coding assistant for Claude that works with code-review-graph and other MCP tools.

name: Cursor
description: AI-powered code editor that supports MCP integration and code-review-graph configuration.

name: Windsurf
description: AI coding platform compatible with MCP-based tools like code-review-graph.

name: Zed
description: High-performance code editor with MCP support for AI-assisted coding workflows.

name: Continue
description: Open-source AI code assistant plugin framework supporting MCP integration.

name: blast-radius analysis
description: Static analysis technique that computes the minimal set of code affected by a change for precise context selection.

name: Episodic Memory
description: Mechanism for tracking code modifications incrementally via git diffs and SHA-256 hashing to update knowledge graphs efficiently.

name: Knowledge Graph
description: Persistent structural map of a codebase represented as nodes (functions, classes) and edges (calls, inheritance, test coverage).

name: GraphRAG
description: Retrieval-augmented generation approach using knowledge graphs to enable precise context selection for LLM queries.

name: Token efficiency
description: Optimization technique for reducing LLM token consumption through selective context provision based on relevance analysis.

name: AST
description: Tree representation of the syntactic structure of source code used for static analysis and dependency tracking.

name: Monorepo
description: Large code repository containing multiple projects where code-review-graph reduces token waste through selective context.

name: Static analysis
description: Technique for analyzing code structure without execution to identify dependencies and compute blast radius for changes.

name: Incremental indexing
description: Approach to updating knowledge graphs on every file save or git commit by diffing and re-parsing only changed files.

name: MCP integration
description: Framework for exposing code-review-graph functionality to AI assistants via Model Context Protocol tools and resources.

name: Memento
description: A technique that teaches language models to self-manage context by segmenting reasoning into blocks and compressing them into dense mementos, achieving 2-3x KV cache reduction.

name: OpenMementos
description: A dataset of 228K annotated reasoning traces built on OpenThoughts-v3 with block segmentation and memento annotations for training context management.

name: OpenThoughts-v3
description: A collection of high-quality reasoning traces generated by QwQ-32B used as the foundation for creating segmented and annotated training data.

name: vLLM
description: An inference serving framework extended with native block masking support for efficient context management in language models.

name: QwQ-32B
description: A reasoning model used to generate high-quality thinking traces that serve as the basis for the OpenMementos dataset.

name: AI Frontiers
description: A boutique research lab inside Microsoft Research that conducted the Memento research.

name: Qwen2.5-7B
description: A language model evaluated in the Memento study for context self-management capabilities.

name: Qwen3-8B
description: A language model tested with Memento training showing peak KV cache reduction and accuracy improvements with reinforcement learning.

name: Qwen3-32B
description: A larger language model evaluated in the Memento research demonstrating context compression benefits.

name: Phi-4-Reasoning
description: A 14B parameter reasoning model tested with Memento for context self-management.

name: Olmo3-7B-Think
description: A language model included in the Memento evaluation across multiple reasoning benchmarks.

name: KV cache
description: The key-value cache memory used during transformer inference that Memento reduces by 2-3x through learned compression.

name: Chain-of-thought
description: The sequential reasoning process that language models generate token-by-token, which Memento learns to compress mid-generation.

name: Context management
description: The skill of determining what information to retain or compress during reasoning, which Memento teaches models to learn during training.

name: Block masking
description: The technique of masking completed reasoning blocks from attention and flushing their KV cache entries after generating mementos.

name: Memento (concept)
description: A compact, information-dense compression of a reasoning block's conclusions, key values, formulas, and decisions that enables future reasoning continuation.

name: Supervised fine-tuning
description: The training approach using ~30K examples to teach language models to segment and compress their own reasoning.

name: Reinforcement learning
description: Training method used to further close accuracy gaps and improve Memento-trained models beyond supervised fine-tuning.

name: Dynamic programming
description: An optimization technique used to place block boundaries in reasoning traces by maximizing boundary quality while balancing block sizes.
